explanations.22: This creates a type alias called JsonNode that can be either a dictionary, a list, or any other type—it's used to describe the flexible structure of JSON data.
explanations.31: The cast() function tells the type checker to treat this variable as a dictionary, even though we already know it is one; it silences warnings from strict linters.
explanations.47: This line keeps the first word lowercase and capitalizes the first letter of every following word (e.g., 'hello_world' becomes 'helloWorld').
explanations.60: The get() method safely looks up the '$ref' key without crashing if it doesn't exist; if it doesn't, it returns None.
explanations.79: Using .get() with a default empty dictionary means if 'components' doesn't exist in the schema, we'll just work with an empty dict instead of crashing.
explanations.85: The pop() method removes the old key from the dictionary and returns its value, then we store that value under the new key name—this effectively renames it.
explanations.102: The cast() function tells the type checker to treat this value as a string, even though we already checked it exists in the operationId field.
explanations.143: We're assigning our custom function directly to app.openapi, which overrides FastAPI's default OpenAPI generator; the type: ignore comment tells the linter not to complain about this unusual assignment.
